Step 1: Determine the original area of the photo.
The original dimensions of the photo are 20 cm by 25 cm. Therefore, the original area \(A_{\text{original}}\) is:
\[
A_{\text{original}} = 20 \times 25 = 500 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 2: Determine the new dimensions of the enlarged photo.
The new dimensions of the enlarged photo are 25 cm by 30 cm. Therefore, the new area \(A_{\text{new}}\) is:
\[
A_{\text{new}} = 25 \times 30 = 750 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 3: Calculate the increase in area.
The increase in area \(\Delta A\) is the difference between the new area and the original area:
\[
\Delta A = A_{\text{new}} - A_{\text{original}} = 750 - 500 = 250 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 4: Calculate the percentage increase in area.
The percentage increase in area is given by the formula:
\[
\text{Percentage Increase} = \left( \frac{\Delta A}{A_{\text{original}}} \right) \times 100 = \left( \frac{250}{500} \right) \times 100 = 50\%
\]

Therefore, the percentage increase in area is \(\boxed{50\%}\). The correct choice is \(\boxed{B}\).

Step 1: Calculate the original area of the photo.
The original dimensions of the photo are 20 cm by 25 cm.
\[
\text{Original Area} = 20 \, \text{cm} \times 25 \, \text{cm} = 500 \, \text{cm}^2
\]

Step 2: Calculate the new area of the enlarged photo.
The new dimensions of the photo are 25 cm by 30 cm.
\[
\text{New Area} = 25 \, \text{cm} \times 30 \, \text{cm} = 750 \, \text{cm}^2
\]

Step 3: Determine the increase in area.
\[
\text{Increase in Area} = \text{New Area} - \text{Original Area} = 750 \, \text{cm}^2 - 500 \, \text{cm}^2 = 250 \, \text{cm}^2
\]

Step 4: Calculate the percentage increase in area.
\[
\text{Percentage Increase} = \left( \frac{\text{Increase in Area}}{\text{Original Area}} \right) \times 100\% = \left( \frac{250 \, \text{cm}^2}{500 \, \text{cm}^2} \right) \times 100\% = 50\%
\]

Conclusion:
\[
\boxed{B}
\]

Step 1: Calculate the area of the original photo.
The original photo has dimensions 20 cm by 25 cm.
\[
\text{Area}_{\text{original}} = 20 \times 25 = 500 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 2: Calculate the area of the enlarged photo.
The enlarged photo has dimensions 25 cm by 30 cm.
\[
\text{Area}_{\text{enlarged}} = 25 \times 30 = 750 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 3: Determine the increase in area.
\[
\text{Increase in area} = \text{Area}_{\text{enlarged}} - \text{Area}_{\text{original}} = 750 - 500 = 250 \text{ cm}^2
\]

Step 4: Calculate the percentage increase in area.
\[
\text{Percentage increase} = \left( \frac{\text{Increase in area}}{\text{Area}_{\text{original}}} \right) \times 100 = \left( \frac{250}{500} \right) \times 100 = 50\%
\]

Conclusion:
\[
\boxed{B}
\]
